question: What component is located beneath the modem?

text_chunks:
23. Split PoE Injector wire and connect to web relay Relay 1. The C terminal goes to the
distribution block. The NO terminal goes to the PoE injector.
24. Complete the power wiring for the modem cable. The white, red, and green wires go to the
positive distribution block. The black wire goes to the negative distribution block.
25. TURN OFF ALL BREAKERS.
26. Complete continuity check procedure DC004.
27. Attach modem to mezzanine plate using QTY two (2) 10-32 socket head cap screws.
28. Attach modem to 4-40 hex standoffs using QTY four 4-40 socket head cap screws.
29. Install CAT5 cables between the modem, web relay, and the Netgear switch. Attach power
cable to modem
11. Load the WAGO DIN rail carriers which will function as distribution blocks. Install two (2) 5-
position WAGOs (PN 221-415) into one carrier (PN 221-501) and a single (1) 5-position WAGO
into the second carrier. The WAGOs should audibly snap into the DIN carrier.
12. Snap the WAGO distribution blocks onto the left side of the DIN rail. Mark the block with two
WAGOs as “+” and the other as “-“ using a permanent marker.
13. Install QTY eight (8) 2” x 4-40 hex standoffs for web relay and modem mezzanine plate.
18. Complete power wiring for Netgear switch with one of the barrel jack connectors. Connect
the barrel jack into the netgear switch and the other end to the distribution block (the wire you
marked with red goes into the + distribution block and the other goes in the - distribution block).
19. Complete power wiring for PoE Injector. Leave extra slack in the PoE injector positive wire
as it will be split for the web relay later.
30. Wrap the strap around the aluminum plate and place the aluminum plate into the enclosure.
Attach with included mounting screws. Secure mounting strap by buckling it around the plate.
31. Connect antenna to CELLULAR SMA jack on modem and tighten with a pliers or wrench.
32. Apply a zip tie and label with “PV IN” around the PV+/- pigtail wires.
33. Attach 10FT outdoor ethernet cable to DATA OUT of PoE injector and coil inside enclosure.
Retain with zip tie for shipping.

DC005[W] FLVM Wire Lengths
altumint.atlassian.net
Controlled Document
Document ID: DC005
Revision: 01
The following wire lengths are recommended for the assembly of the Flashing Light View
Monitor electronics enclosure. Lengths are permitted to be adjusted by the production team to
improve routing and assembly.
All names are the same as are used on the wiring diagram. The only exception are the
highlighted wires in Figure 1 below. These will be referred to as BATT JUMPER BLACK and 
BATT JUMPER RED in the wire length table.
18AWG, 90C, RED Wires

TM001(02) Flashing Light Video Monitor Assembly Instructions
Materials Required
Procedure
Kitting Instructions
FLVM Assembly Instructions 2-4-2026.docx
Materials Required
1. 18 AWG 90C wire (red and black)
2. Zip ties
3. Label maker
4. WAGO 221-415 5-position lever nuts
5. WAGO 221-612 2-position lever nuts (10 AWG)
6. Wire cutter/snips
7. 3/32” hex drive bit
8. 1/8” hex drive bit
9. 5/32 hex drive bit
10. Precision flat head screwdriver
11. Small and large Phillips screwdriver
Procedure
1. Drill holes in enclosure for antenna, 2x vents, and 1” NPT conduit fitting.
2. Install the antenna base into the top enclosure hole. Be sure to lubricate the o-ring before
tightening.
